name: cairn
description: Token-efficient agent harness with tool readiness for Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity.

interface.shortDescription: Memory, planning, tool readiness, and two-gate verification harness for agent teams.
interface.longDescription: Cairn brings a LazyCodex-inspired flow to Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity while reducing token use through repository-local MEMORY.md, PLAN.md, domain notes, tool readiness checks, module-task execution, and two-gate verification.
defaultPrompt: Use Cairn to create MEMORY.md and PLAN.md for this repository.
At the start of each task, every agent must read the project-root MEMORY.md for domain knowledge and repository policy before planning, implementation, review, or delegation.
Run cairn-toolcheck before planning implementation work.
Before non-trivial implementation, run cairn-plan for this task without asking me for discoverable facts.
When planning, first understand the whole work, then classify it into small executable tasks; if a task needs further breakdown, split it into sub-tasks.

The user-called/main agent is the orchestrator: plan, assign, verify, and record evidence; actual implementation edits must be delegated to worker subagents whenever subagent tools are available, on both Light Path and Heavy Path.

When the subagent tool provides a progress-reporting channel, subagents report status to the orchestrator when starting work, when deciding or confirming direction, during periodic progress, and when finishing; the orchestrator must immediately relay received status events to the user. If no mid-run reporting channel exists, the orchestrator relays observable events such as assignment, waiting, and final completion.

When a delegated subagent finishes, it must provide its final report before leaving; after the orchestrator captures the final report and evidence, the orchestrator must close or release the completed subagent, then review the final report and evidence before marking the work complete.
When subagent tools are available, each agent may recursively delegate bounded sub-tasks to subagents; every child subagent must read MEMORY.md, keep scope, and preserve others' edits.

If subagent tools are unavailable, the main agent takes over implementation directly and records that takeover in evidence; do not stop before local implementation solely because subagent tools are unavailable.
Record Light Path or Heavy Path complexity triage in docs/plan/<topic>.md before mutating files.
Run cairn-work from the current PLAN.md and pass dry-run/check, module acceptance, and surface integration gates.

If I ask a side question, status question, or narrow clarification while a plan or task is still active, answer it briefly and then resume the previous active work unless I explicitly ask you to pause, stop, or switch tasks.
